info.title: SqlInliner
info.description: Helper utility to inline SQL server database views

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription:
SQL inliner v2.4.1+1b8a1e5658f2e645e5956f382ef654036374aa1e - https://github.com/stevehansen/sql-inliner/ — Optimizes SQL Server views by inlining nested views into a single flattened query.

At least one of --view-name or --view-path is required.
When both are supplied, --view-path provides the main view while nested views are fetched from the database via --connection-string.

Examples:
  sqlinliner -cs "Server=.;Database=Test;Integrated Security=true" -vn "dbo.VHeavy" --strip-unused-joins
  sqlinliner -vp "./views/MyView.sql" --strip-unused-joins
  sqlinliner -vp "./views/MyView.sql" --generate-create-or-alter false
  sqlinliner -c sqlinliner.json -vn dbo.VHeavy

Join hints — annotate JOINs in your SQL to enable safe join removal:
  LEFT JOIN /* @join:unique */ dbo.Address a ON a.PersonId = p.Id
  INNER JOIN /* @join:unique @join:required */ dbo.Status s ON s.Id = p.StatusId

For programmatic usage, install the SqlInliner.Library NuGet package:
  dotnet add package SqlInliner.Library

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: --config
options.description: Path to a sqlinliner.json configuration file. Auto-discovers sqlinliner.json in the current directory if not specified

options.name: --connection-string
options.description: Connection string to the SQL Server database

options.name: --view-name
options.description: Fully qualified name of the view to inline (e.g. dbo.MyView)

options.name: --view-path
options.description: Path to a .sql file containing a CREATE VIEW statement

options.name: --strip-unused-columns
options.description: Remove columns from nested views that the outer view does not reference [default: True]

options.name: --strip-unused-joins
options.description: Remove joins whose tables contribute no columns to the result. Use @join:unique / @join:required hints on JOINs to allow safe removal (see README) [default: False]

options.name: --aggressive-join-stripping
options.description: Exclude join-condition column references from the usage count. Allows stripping joins where the table only appears in its own ON clause. Use with care: can change results for INNER JOINs where the ON clause filters rows [default: False]

options.name: --flatten-derived-tables
options.description: Flatten derived tables (subqueries) produced by inlining into the outer query. Experimental: handles single-table and multi-table (JOIN) subqueries without GROUP BY, HAVING, TOP, DISTINCT, or UNION [default: False]

options.name: --generate-create-or-alter
options.description: Wrap output in a CREATE OR ALTER VIEW statement [default: True]

options.name: --output-path
options.description: Write the resulting SQL to a file instead of the console

options.name: --log-path
options.description: Write warnings, errors, and timing info to a file

commands.name: optimize
commands.description: Interactive optimization wizard: inline, deploy, validate, and benchmark a view against a backup database

options.name: --connection-string
options.description: Connection string to the SQL Server database. Can also be provided via config file.

options.name: --view-name
options.description: Fully qualified view name (e.g. dbo.VPeople). If omitted, you will be prompted.

commands.name: validate
commands.description: Batch-validate all views: inline each view and report pass/fail, optionally deploy and run COUNT + EXCEPT comparisons

options.name: --connection-string
options.description: Connection string to the SQL Server database. Can also be provided via config file.

options.name: --deploy
options.description: Deploy each inlined view and run COUNT + EXCEPT validation against the original. [default: False]

options.name: --deploy-only
options.description: Deploy each inlined view to check for SQL errors, but skip COUNT + EXCEPT comparison (faster than --deploy). [default: False]

options.name: --output-dir
options.description: Save inlined SQL files to a directory.

options.name: --stop-on-error
options.description: Halt on first failure instead of continuing through all views. [default: False]

options.name: --filter
options.description: Only process matching views. Supports exact name or SQL LIKE-style % wildcard (e.g. dbo.V%).

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Query timeout in seconds for COUNT and EXCEPT queries (default: 90). [default: 90]

options.name: --strip-unused-columns
options.description: Remove columns from nested views that the outer view does not reference. [default: True]

options.name: --strip-unused-joins
options.description: Remove joins whose tables contribute no columns to the result. [default: False]

options.name: --aggressive-join-stripping
options.description: Exclude join-condition column references from the usage count. [default: False]

options.name: --flatten-derived-tables
options.description: Flatten derived tables (subqueries) produced by inlining into the outer query. [default: False]

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Verify deployed inlined views against their embedded originals: auto-detect markers, deploy original as temp view, run COUNT + EXCEPT comparisons

options.name: --connection-string
options.description: Connection string to the SQL Server database. Can also be provided via config file.

options.name: --filter
options.description: Only process matching views. Supports exact name or SQL LIKE-style % wildcard (e.g. dbo.V%).

options.name: --stop-on-error
options.description: Halt on first failure instead of continuing through all views. [default: False]

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Query timeout in seconds for COUNT and EXCEPT queries (default: 120). [default: 120]

commands.name: analyze
commands.description: Analyze views to identify inlining candidates: ranks by nesting depth, Query Store stats, and inlined status

options.name: --connection-string
options.description: Connection string to the SQL Server database. Can also be provided via config file.

options.name: --filter
options.description: Only process matching views. Supports exact name or SQL LIKE-style % wildcard (e.g. dbo.V%).

options.name: --days
options.description: Query Store lookback period in days (default: 30). [default: 30]

options.name: --min-executions
options.description: Minimum execution count for Query Store stats (default: 5). [default: 5]

options.name: --top
options.description: Limit output to the top N candidates by score.

options.name: --generate-script
options.description: Generate a SQL Server stored procedure for extracting analyze data, instead of running analysis. [default: False]

options.name: --from-file
options.description: Load analyze data from a previously exported JSON file (offline mode, no database required).

options.name: --output-path
options.description: Write output to a file instead of the console (used with --generate-script).

commands.name: credentials
commands.description: Manage stored database credentials in the OS credential store

commands.name: add
commands.description: Store credentials for a server/database pair

options.name: --server
options.description: SQL Server hostname or instance name

options.name: --database
options.description: Database name

options.name: --username
options.description: Username (prompted if not provided)

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all stored credentials (passwords are never shown)

commands.name: remove
commands.description: Remove stored credentials for a server/database pair

options.name: --server
options.description: SQL Server hostname or instance name

options.name: --database
options.description: Database name
